description: Decode-time (T==1) Q4_K weight-GEMV for FFN gate/up, FFN down, and attn q/o projection. Covers the owned warp GEMV route (q4k_gemv_warp_kernel) and the scheduler-expressible coop / vdot variants.
exposed_instruction_primitives: udot4 / v_dot4 builtin (Q4K_VDOT) -- schedulable, NATIVE; but loses in-model 0.96x due to per-kernel q8 activation-quant cost (~7us)
exposed_scheduling_primitives: workgroup_shape (threads/row, K-block parallelism via coop rt)
exposed_dataflow_primitives: K-block split + partial sum (coop: q4k_coop_partial + sum)
exposed_runtime_primitives: per-role flag gating (Q4K_GEMV_WARP, Q4K_GEMV_WARP_DOWN, Q4K_GEMV_WARP_PROJ, Q4K_ATTN_QO_COOP, Q4K_VDOT)

newly_exposed_primitives.status: EXPOSED opt-in 2026-06-25 (WARP_REDUCE_LOWERING, default OFF) -- a generic Ops.REDUCE over a pow2 (<=32) WARP/GROUP_REDUCE lane axis auto-lowers to the ds_bpermute ladder (drops LDS+barrier), correct (sum/max). Was 'excluded / no renderer lowering' before.
newly_exposed_primitives.remaining_to_default_on: mixed reduce (serial-K + group, the real GEMV K=4096 shape) still declines -> LDS; then W==D vs owned warp GEMV (Milestone 6)

excluded_primitives.primitive: cross_lane_reduction (mixed serial+group reduce, e.g. real GEMV K=4096)
excluded_primitives.reason: pm_warp_reduce first pass handles only a single full-group lane axis; a K>group reduce (group + serial-K/group) still falls to the LDS tree. The serial-then-ladder split is the M6 follow-on.
excluded_primitives.used_by_winning_kernel: q4k_gemv_warp_kernel (extra/q4_k_gemv_primitive.py) -- shipped default-on for gate/up + down + (2026-06-25) q/o proj

correctness_gate: byte_exact (lossless work-decomposition; greedy 0 mismatches vs default)
lifecycle_authority_gate: clean W==D (extra/qk_decode_runtime_overhead.py) interleaved drift-cancelled A/B; gate >= +5%@ctx1024 OR +7%@ctx4096 (FFN) / positive byte-identical (proj)
classification_detail: Cross-lane reduction is now scheduler-emittable for both single-group AND mixed serial+group reduces (real K=4096), opt-in (WARP_REDUCE_LOWERING), correct -- the cross-lane codegen gap that blocked search is RESOLVED. Milestone 6 (2026-06-25) WIRED a scheduler-generated FFN gate/up GEMV (Q4K_GEMV_SCHEDULER) and W==D-compared it to the owned warp kernel: it TRAILS ~2x AND cross-lane is ~neutral vs the LDS tree (-0.1..-0.3%) because the reduce is NOT the bottleneck of a bandwidth-bound GEMV. So the search-vs-oracle gap is NO LONGER the reduce/cross-lane -- it is the Q4_K dequant lifecycle + coalesced packed-word loads + block-group-K. Those are the next search-space levers.

coalesced_packed_word_experiment_2026_06.result: A word-structured packed-Q4_K dequant matvec (Q4K_GEMV_SCHEDULER=2) was built (layout byte-exact vs gguf, tokens match owned) and measured: ~22 tok/s = ~4.6x off owned, WORSE than the fp16-logical scheduler arm (~50). For the large gate/up output the heuristic does output-parallel + serial-K with STRIDED per-row word loads (uncoalesced); restructuring the dequant expression cannot impose the owned kernel's per-row-workgroup + word-col-lane thread-map, and the lowerer does not synthesize a coalesced packed-word load from the gather.
coalesced_packed_word_experiment_2026_06.conclusion: The packed-word COALESCING is NOT reachable in pure scheduler Tensor ops / by MV opts -- it is a CUSTOM/codegen capability. Closes 'can a pure scheduler GEMV match owned' = NO.

a_vs_b_resolution_2026_06_25.question: is the coalescing gap (A) search-navigation [optimum in space, not found] or (B) representation-incomplete [optimum not in space]
a_vs_b_resolution_2026_06_25.recognition_gap_A: REAL but shallow: the matvec detector (heuristic.py:67) needs reduceop.src[0]==MUL(INDEX,INDEX); a fused dequant is MUL(dequant(INDEX(words)),INDEX(x)) so it is missed -> no GROUP -> output-parallel. Fixed via gated MV_DEQUANT (sees through the dequant, applies GROUP).
a_vs_b_resolution_2026_06_25.result: MV_DEQUANT fires + applies GROUP(32) to the gate/up K-reduce (DEBUG-confirmed) but yields NO speedup: owned 103.5 / fp16_def 50.9 / fp16_mvdq32(GROUP) 49.9 / fp16_mvdq8 9.8.
a_vs_b_resolution_2026_06_25.verdict: CONSTRUCTIVE (B): the coalescing opt (GROUP) exists, recognition is fixable, GROUP can be applied -- yet the owned kernel is unreachable by the exposed knobs even when forced. Not navigation; a genuine representation/codegen incompleteness. The owned coalesced-packed-word + in-register-8-nibble-dequant + block-group-K structure is not expressible as (reshape + GROUP) over the standard dequant graph.

layout_ir_2026_06_25.phase_b_reshuffle_sidestep: TESTED + INSUFFICIENT. Marlin-style 'arrange data/access so coalescing is already correct': restructured the scheduler GEMV 3 ways reading the same contiguous packed words -- mode1(fp16-logical) 50, mode2(word-256-order) 22, mode3(word-axis-on-lane + forced GROUP) 14 tok/s, all << owned 103. Forcing GROUP(32) directly (MV_DEQUANT) = no speedup (49.9). VERDICT: coalescing is NECESSARY BUT NOT SUFFICIENT -- the owned win is the whole kernel structure (one packed word/lane + in-register 8-nibble dequant + block-group-K + single accumulate/reduce/store), not just a coalesced load. The data-reshuffle sidestep does NOT close a pure-scheduler GEMV.
layout_ir_2026_06_25.phase_a_layout_ir: DESIGNED (docs/layout-mapping-ir-design-20260625.md, CuTe/LinearLayout/Hexcute-grounded; fork already deleted ShapeTracker/View so layout = RANGE/INDEX algebra, IR is additive: LayoutFn + composition + LaneMap (generalize TensorCore.swizzle) + Ops.LAYOUT_TRANSFORM + OptOps.COALESCE + anchored propagation). SEED shipped: extra/qk_layout_coalesce_check.py -- a STATIC coalescing predicate (stride=addr(lane+1)-addr(lane) over the index algebra), test/external/test_layout_coalesce_check.py. Milestones M0-M4; full IR multi-month; owned-kernel SPEED ultimately needs the M4 codegen layer (coalesced packed-word load the lowerer can exploit + v_dot2/cross-lane lowerings), not just the representation.
layout_ir_2026_06_25.full_scope: docs/layout-codegen-full-scope-20260625.md (P0-P4 roadmap, dep graph, gates, kill-criteria; ~16-22wk critical-path-to-M-E decision gate; keystone = LaneMap-aware add_gpudims; honest end-state Tier-2 ~80-95% of owned, CUSTOM the last-mile ceiling)

next_search_space_levers: CODEGEN CAPABILITY (the real gap, B-proven): a coalesced packed-uint32-word load the lowerer can exploit (one word/lane, in-register multi-nibble dequant) + the owned block-group-K thread-map; OR a Marlin-style OFFLINE WEIGHT RESHUFFLE so a plain GROUP coalesces. Not reachable by reshape+GROUP/opts (constructively proven 2026-06-25).

MV_DEQUANT (shipped, gated/default-off): matvec-detection sees through fused dequant -> applies GROUP. Fixes the recognition gap (may help other fused-reduce shapes); does NOT close the GEMV (proof apparatus for the B verdict).
OR pivot: v_dot2 renderer lowering for the attention tile (the other hand kernel)
promotion_status_note: Owned warp GEMV is shipped default-on (search_space_id=manual_oracle_not_search_generated). Q4K_GEMV_SCHEDULER + WARP_REDUCE_LOWERING are research-only/default-off; a scheduler GEMV does not yet challenge the owned kernel (the gap is dequant/memory, not the reduce).
